evals.name: slow-checkout-troubleshoot
evals.prompt: Customers have been complaining that our checkout page hangs for several seconds before completing. I grabbed a short client-side capture during one of the slow moments — it's at /sessions/sweet-trusting-darwin/mnt/pcap_analysis_sandbox/pcaps/eval-slow-app.pcap. Can you tell me what's going on and what to do about it? A written analysis I can hand to our network team would be ideal.
evals.expected_output: Structured markdown report. Must identify: a small number of retransmissions affecting one TCP stream, at least one zero-window event, the RTT between client and server (~120ms), and frame references for each finding. Should NOT conclude 'nothing wrong'.

evals.name: dns-cascade-troubleshoot
evals.prompt: Ops is getting 502 errors from our checkout service and nobody knows why. Here's the capture from the checkout host: /sessions/sweet-trusting-darwin/mnt/pcap_analysis_sandbox/pcaps/eval-dns-cascade.pcap. Is this a DNS problem, a backend problem, or both? I need a short writeup for the incident ticket.
evals.expected_output: Structured markdown report. Must identify: NXDOMAIN responses for some names (newsvc.*), successful resolution for others (api.prod, auth.prod), HTTP 502 responses from the backend that DID resolve. Should distinguish 'DNS failed' from 'backend returned 502' and point to both as contributing factors.

evals.name: beacon-security-ir
evals.prompt: SOC flagged workstation 10.0.4.10 for unusual behavior. I pulled a ~20 minute capture from their switch span port: /sessions/sweet-trusting-darwin/mnt/pcap_analysis_sandbox/pcaps/eval-beacon.pcap. Can you take a look and tell me if there's anything concerning? If so I need IOCs I can feed into our SIEM.
evals.expected_output: Structured markdown report in security/IR mode. Must identify: periodic ~60s-interval small TCP/443 sessions from 10.0.4.10 to 185.220.101.45, the DNS name c2-check.examplebad.xyz that resolves to that IP, consistent small payload sizes. Should include an IOC table (IP, domain, and ideally port/pattern). Should distinguish this from the normal-looking traffic to the other external host.
